body:
## Problem

Both jobs in `.github/workflows/repository-hardening.yml` were failing on every push:

| Job | Root cause |
|-----|-----------|
| `secret-scan` | `gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2` requires a `GITLEAKS_LICENSE` org secret. The secret is not configured on `Dealix-sa`, so the action aborts before scanning. |
| `filesystem-scan` | `aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0` no longer exists. Tags 0.0.1–0.34.2 were deleted during the March 2026 supply-chain remediation (all those tags had been hijacked to steal CI secrets). The setup step therefore fails immediately. |

Neither failure was caused by feature code — they were pre-existing infra issues.

## Fix

### Gitleaks (`secret-scan`)

Replace `gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2` with a direct install of the MIT-licensed Gitleaks CLI binary (v8.30.1). The CLI requires no license key regardless of org visibility. SARIF output is saved and uploaded to the GitHub Security tab via `github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3` on `if: always()`, so detected secrets still appear in the Security tab even if the scan step itself fails.

### Trivy (`filesystem-scan`)

Bump `aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0` → `@0.36.0`. v0.36.0 (April 2026) is the latest release and is protected by GitHub's immutable releases feature, making it safe from the tag-hijacking attack that affected all prior tags. All other scan parameters (severity, exit-code, format) are unchanged.

## Test plan

- [ ] `secret-scan` job runs to completion with no `GITLEAKS_LICENSE` secret present
- [ ] `filesystem-scan` job runs to completion — action setup succeeds at `@0.36.0`
- [ ] Both jobs green on a clean repo (no secrets, no critical/high CVEs in deps)
- [ ] SARIF report appears in the repo's Security → Code scanning tab after a successful run

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WsPg3YP4Ectc3CqpqqJeAF

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code/session_01WsPg3YP4Ectc3CqpqqJeAF)_

comments.body:
You are seeing this message because GitHub Code Scanning has recently been set up for this repository, or this pull request contains the workflow file for the Code Scanning tool.

### What Enabling Code Scanning Means:

- The 'Security' tab will display more code scanning analysis results (e.g., for the default branch).
- Depending on your configuration and choice of analysis tool, future pull requests will be annotated with code scanning analysis results.
- You will be able to see the analysis results for the pull request's branch on this [overview](/Dealix-sa/dealix/security/code-scanning?query=pr%3A598+is%3Aopen) once the scans have completed and the checks have passed.

For more information about GitHub Code Scanning, check out [the documentation](https://docs.github.com/code-security/code-scanning/introduction-to-code-scanning/about-code-scanning).

commits.messageBody:
- Gitleaks: switch from gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2 (requires
  GITLEAKS_LICENSE org secret) to direct CLI install of v8.30.1
  (MIT-licensed, no license key required). SARIF report uploaded
  via github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3 on always() so leaks
  surface in Security tab even when the step fails.

- Trivy: bump from @0.24.0 to @0.36.0. Tags 0.0.1–0.34.2 were
  deleted during the March-2026 supply-chain remediation, which
  is why @0.24.0 was causing setup failures. v0.36.0 is the first
  post-remediation release protected by immutable releases.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WsPg3YP4Ectc3CqpqqJeAF
commits.messageHeadline: fix(ci): replace broken hardening workflow actions

title: fix(ci): repair broken hardening workflow (Gitleaks + Trivy)
